content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print the grades of 3 students based on the marks they have obtained provided as input. The student is graded **A**  if marks are greater than **90**, **B** if marks are greater than **70**, **C** if greater than or equal to **40**, else **F**.

[**Note:** You need to take inputs for all 3 students.]
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, three lines follow, each containing an integer mark of a student (1 ≤ mark ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output three characters (A, B, C or F), each on its own line, representing the grades of the three students in the order they were given.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All marks are exactly 40, satisfying the >=40 condition, so each grade is C.

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.expectedOutput:
C
B
A
F
C
C

solution.approach:
For every mark we apply the grading rules directly:
- If mark > 90 → grade A
- Else if mark > 70 → grade B
- Else if mark >= 40 → grade C
- Else → grade F
We repeat this for the three marks of each test case and print the resulting grades line by line.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – we examine exactly three marks per test case, so the total work is proportional to the number of test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used regardless of input size.
